texto: Perhaps we are like cells in the mind of God, contributing to celestial functions beyond our ken, just as our cells unknowingly fashion our thoughts and actions. Perhaps the laws of nature order and constrain even the living and eternal Deity from which they spring, and of which they are a part.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Robert Christian: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “Perhaps we are like cells in the mind of God, contributing to celestial functions beyond our ken,…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, deity, humanistic pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e humanistic.
